universal_credit_childcare_element.eligibility: Both parents working (or one working and one with LCWRA/carer status in specific cases). Costs must be with a registered provider.
universal_credit_childcare_element.payment_method: Reimbursement after costs are paid and reported. Help with upfront costs available via Flexible Support Fund or Budgeting Advance.

tax_free_childcare.mechanic: Government adds £2 for every £8 paid into the account.
tax_free_childcare.disabled_child_definition: Receiving DLA, PIP, or certified blind.
tax_free_childcare.eligibility: Both parents working (or single parent), each earning at least £190/week (approx 16 hrs at NMW) and less than £100,000/year.
tax_free_childcare.interaction: MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with Universal Credit. You cannot get both at the same time.

working_parent_entitlement.eligibility_income_floor: 16 hours/week at National Minimum Wage (approx £2,643 per quarter)
working_parent_entitlement.eligibility_income_ceiling: Under £100,000 adjusted net income per parent

sen_specific_15_hours.eligibility: Child receives DLA or has an EHCP (Education, Health and Care Plan). Not dependent on parent work status.

disability_access_fund_daf.description: Grant paid directly to the childcare provider to help them make reasonable adjustments for a child on DLA.

hmrc_mileage_rates.cars_and_vans: 45p per mile for first 10,000 miles, 25p thereafter

weekly_commuting_calculation_example.note: This is a real-world cost that reduces the parent's actual net gain from working.

public_transport.typical_costs: Variable by region. Users should input their monthly/weekly season ticket price.
public_transport.note: Public transport costs are not deductible from earnings for UC taper calculations (except for some self-employed expenses).

flexible_support_fund_fsf.description: Discretionary fund managed by local Jobcentres to help with the costs of getting into work.
allowable_costs: Travel costs for the first month of a new job until the first paycheck
flexible_support_fund_fsf.eligibility: Must be claiming a qualifying benefit (e.g. UC) and have the expense approved by a Work Coach.

access_to_work_travel_support.eligibility: Disabled workers (including parents with their own disabilities) who cannot use public transport.
access_to_work_travel_support.support: Can cover taxi fares to work or adaptations to vehicles.
access_to_work_travel_support.benefit_interaction: Grants are not counted as income and do not reduce UC or other benefits.

better_off_calculation_notes.formula: Net Gain = (Gross Pay - Tax - NI - Pension) + (UC Elements - (Earnings - Work Allowance) * 0.55) - Childcare Gap (15% or more) - Commuting Costs
better_off_calculation_notes.critical_choice: Families must choose between UC Childcare Element (85% rebate) and Tax-Free Childcare (25% top-up). UC is generally better for lower/middle earners.
